name: basic-threadhijack+sysmon
description: Thread-execution hijacking (MITRE T1055.003) observed under both providers. Critical thesis cell: thread hijacking does NOT call CreateRemoteThread (the hijacked thread already exists), so Sysmon Event 8 should NOT fire. Sysmon Event 10 (ProcessAccess) WILL fire because ProcessToolkit needs an OpenProcess handle with PROCESS_VM_WRITE | THREAD_SUSPEND_RESUME to redirect RIP. Distinguishes thread hijacking from CreateRemoteThread-based injection at the Sysmon layer.

extra.notes: Suspends an existing thread, redirects RIP to a stub that calls LoadLibraryW and returns to the original RIP. Expected Sysmon profile: ProcessAccess yes, CreateRemoteThread NO.
